findings.title: optparse module is deprecated
findings.message: The optparse module was deprecated in Python 2.7 and removed in Python 3. It has been superseded by argparse, which provides a more powerful and flexible command-line argument parsing interface.
findings.suggestion: Use `import argparse` instead. The argparse module provides the same functionality with a more consistent API, automatic help generation, and better error messages.

findings.title: Deserializing untrusted pickle data
findings.message: pickle.loads() and pickle.load() can execute arbitrary code when deserializing untrusted data.
findings.suggestion: Never deserialize pickle data from untrusted sources. Use JSON or a safe serialization format instead.

findings.title: Shell injection via subprocess with shell=True
findings.message: Using shell=True with subprocess functions can lead to shell injection if the command string contains user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Use shell=False with a list of arguments, or sanitize input with shlex.quote().

findings.title: Package does not exist on PyPI
findings.message: The package 'optparse' does not exist on the PyPI registry. This is a common AI hallucination pattern where an LLM invents a package name that sounds plausible but doesn't exist.
findings.suggestion: Verify the package name at https://pypi.org/optparse. If you need similar functionality, search for real packages with comparable names. If this is an internal package, ensure it is published to your private registry.
